node.statement: N1.2 (spectral idempotents intertwine: Ftilde j = j P). By GT-hyp, F=jC and Phi=Cj, so Fj=jCj=j(Cj)=jPhi; thus j intertwines F and Phi (Fj=jPhi). By GT-hyp j is a unital embedding (j(I)=I), hence bounded and unital. By N1.1, ||Phi^2-Phi||<1/4 and ||F^2-F||<1/4 (both spectral idempotents well-defined). Therefore GT-intertwine (lem-intertwine-spectral-idempotent) applies with this F, Phi, j: theta(2F-1) j = j theta(2Phi-1), i.e. Ftilde j = j P (writing Ftilde=theta(2F-1), P=theta(2Phi-1) per GT-hyp). The op-norm statement of GT-intertwine suffices; cb->op passage is harmless since ||.||_op<=||.||_cb.
